definitions.scope: Mandatory and voluntary sustainability reporting frameworks, climate-related financial disclosure, ESG governance requirements (board oversight, assurance, data integrity). Governance is included here because regulatory treatment of ESG governance is primarily through disclosure obligations (TCFD governance pillar, ISSB S2 governance requirements, board-level accountability provisions).
definitions.examples: ISSB S1/S2 local adoption, CSRD/ESRS, SGX 711A/B, HKEX climate rules, TCFD-aligned mandates.

definitions.scope: Financial risk from the policy and market shift to a low-carbon economy. Regulations requiring entities to plan for, quantify, or manage the risk that assets lose value as decarbonisation standards tighten — stranded asset exposure, carbon pricing, CRREM pathway exceedance, lender/insurer repricing, transition plan mandates.
definitions.examples: MAS Transition Planning Guidelines, CRREM pathways, IIGCC PCRAM, green/transition taxonomy requirements.

definitions.scope: Financial risk from physical climate impacts on the asset. Regulations addressing flood, heat, cyclone, bushfire, sea-level rise, and other climate hazards through building code resilience uplifts, mandatory physical risk disclosure, or planning restrictions.
definitions.examples: Building code resilience uplifts (wind, flood, heat), mandatory physical risk assessment, planning overlays for climate hazards, insurability requirements.

definitions.scope: Whole-building sustainability rating schemes and design standards assessing multiple environmental criteria (energy, water, materials, indoor environment) as an integrated package.
definitions.examples: Green Mark, Green Star, BEAM Plus, BREEAM, CASBEE, G-SEED, GBI, IGBC/GRIHA, TREES, Greenship, LEED.

definitions.scope: Regulations targeting energy consumed and carbon emitted during building operation. Mandatory energy performance codes, minimum efficiency standards for existing buildings, operational energy intensity labelling, building performance standards with emissions caps, and energy audit mandates.
definitions.examples: NABERS, NCC Section J, MEES, Décret Tertiaire, NYC LL97, BCA MEI, ECBC, Building Energy Codes.

definitions.scope: Regulations targeting the carbon embedded in construction materials and the building lifecycle (cradle-to-grave). Whole-life carbon assessments, material-level carbon benchmarks, Environmental Product Declaration (EPD) requirements, low-carbon procurement mandates.
definitions.examples: RE2020 (France), London Plan WLC, Singapore concrete carbon benchmark, EPD mandates.

definitions.scope: Construction and demolition waste regulation, material reuse and recycling mandates, extended producer responsibility schemes, single-use product restrictions affecting retail and commercial tenancies, circular economy requirements.
definitions.examples: Korea waste policy, Singapore Beverage Container Return Scheme, C&D waste diversion mandates.

definitions.scope: Regulations addressing nature loss, biodiversity, urban greening, water efficiency, and ecosystem restoration as they apply to the built environment. TNFD-aligned disclosure, biodiversity net gain requirements, urban greening ratios, water recycling mandates.
definitions.examples: EU Nature Restoration Regulation, UK Biodiversity Net Gain, urban greening ratio requirements.

definitions.scope: Regulations addressing social outcomes in construction and real estate operation: modern slavery and forced labour in supply chains, construction worker safety, community impact, affordable housing linkages, DEI disclosure.
definitions.examples: UK Modern Slavery Act, US UFLPA (solar panel/materials supply chain), construction safety mandates.
